title: Hyperion Distributed Cache Layer
problem: High-load APIs encountered critical bottleneck issues due to slow database read queries, raising latency averages to 4.2 seconds under heavy load.
solution: Engineered a low-latency distributed caching layer written in Go, implementing custom consistent hashing groups and LRU eviction policies.
challenges: Managing data inconsistencies during cache evictions and node scaling. Solved by introducing double-barrier consensus patterns and write-through caching buffers.
lessonsLearned: Learned deep mechanics of socket connections, memory buffers management, and the performance trade-offs of consistent hashing groups.
futureImprovements: Incorporate Raft consensus for active state replication and automate horizontal pod autoscaling in Kubernetes environments.

title: Decoupled Gutenberg Block Sync Plugin
problem: Hiring managers wanted a headless website but struggled to maintain layout designs, as React frontends did not match WordPress block layout structures.
solution: Authored a custom WordPress plugin that maps Gutenberg block JSON data schemas dynamically to Next.js components via WP GraphQL.
challenges: Resolving nested blocks and dynamic block queries in single queries. Solved by writing custom schema resolvers in PHP and a compiler parsing nested block trees in React.
lessonsLearned: Discovered advanced PHP GraphQL schema filters, custom block definitions, and client-side recursive rendering patterns.
futureImprovements: Implement block pre-render caches on the server side and introduce visual live-preview drag and drop support.
